name: Eastern Barrier and Turnstile
description: The primary Passkontrolle gate on the eastern side of the checkpoint — a heavy steel turnstile set into a concrete frame, flanked by two guard booths with bulletproof glass, the whole structure now bending visibly under the sustained pressure of the crowd. The floodlights above cast everything in a flat white that makes the guards' faces unreadable.
atmosphericDetails: The cold steel of the turnstile bar pressing into ribs and shoulders — the physical reality of the barrier as a body-level experience
The sound of thousands of voices merging into a single rhythmic chant, punctuated by individual shouts that spike above the mass and then dissolve back into it
The smell of two-stroke Trabant exhaust mixing with cold fog and the sweat of bodies pressed together in the November air
